q: In our daily life we use different forms of energy such as heat energy, light energy, mechanical energy, electrical energy, chemical energy and ___ energy.
The most common forms of energy are heat, light and ___.
All the sources of energy can be divided into two categories: renewable sources and ___ sources of energy.
A non-renewable resource is a natural substance that is not replenished with the ___ at which it is consumed.
A non-renewable resource is a ___ resource.
Non-renewable energy resources include coal, natural gas, crude oil, and ___ energy.
Non-renewable energy resources occur in limited and ___ quantities.
Non-renewable energy resources cannot be regenerated in a short period of time or used again and ___.
Renewable energy sources (also called non-conventional energy sources) are continuously replenished by ___ processes.
Renewable energy is the energy generated by using wind, solar, hydro, ___, etc.
Renewable energy sources cannot be exhausted easily and can be generated at a ___ rate for their use over and over again.
Non-renewable energy sources are available only in ___ supply.
Fossil fuels (like coal, oil, natural gases, etc.) and nuclear energy are examples of ___ energy sources.
It takes a long time (often millions of years) for ___ resources to be replenished.
Renewable resources are replenished naturally and over ___ periods of time.
Solar energy, hydro energy, wind energy and geothermal energy are examples of ___ resources.
